sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Standard Liège has struggled recently, with only 2 wins in their last 5 matches, including 3 losses. KAA Gent, on the other hand, shows better form with 3 wins in their last 5 matches, displaying offensive strength with key players like Maksim Paskotsi in good shape. Gent’s recent 4-2 victory over Anderlecht highlights their attacking potential.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: KAA Gent has dominated recent encounters, winning all of the last 5 head-to-head matches against Standard Liège, including a convincing 4-0 win in October 2025. Standard Liège has failed to score in their last 3 matches against Gent, indicating a significant challenge ahead.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Standard Liège sits 7th in the league with 27 points, struggling offensively with only 18 goals scored (14th in the league). KAA Gent, positioned 6th with 29 points, has a more potent attack with 32 goals scored, though their defense has conceded 31 goals. Gent’s ability to score late in matches (25.8% of goals in the 76-90 minute range) could be decisive.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: For Standard Liège, Rafiki Saïd (3 goals, 2 assists) will be crucial in breaking Gent’s defense, but their low possession (44.6%) and penalty area entries (15th in league) suggest struggles in attack. Gent’s Wilfried Kanga (5 goals, 3 assists) and Hyllarion Goore (4 goals, 3 assists) pose a significant threat, especially with Gent’s higher final third entries and expected goals per game.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: Given Gent’s superior form, head-to-head dominance, and attacking output, they are favored to win, aligning with Sofascore’s 44% away win probability. Standard Liège’s defensive solidity (6 clean sheets) might keep it close, but their lack of goals against Gent historically tips the balance. Expect a low-scoring but competitive match at Maurice Dufrasne.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: At half-time, KAA Gent leads Standard Liège by a score of 2-0 at the Maurice Dufrasne stadium. Gent's dominance in the first half was evident through clinical finishing, with Wilfried Kanga netting both goals. Standard Liège, despite having more possession and territorial advantage, failed to convert their opportunities into goals. The home side showed intent but lacked the final touch, while Gent capitalized on their fewer chances with precision. This sets up an intriguing second half as Standard Liège will look to mount a comeback.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance and statistics
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège controlled the ball for the majority of the first half and made frequent entries into the final third and penalty area. Their high possession and duel-winning stats indicate a strong physical presence, yet they couldn't translate this into shots on target. On the other hand, KAA Gent made the most of their limited possession, securing two goals from minimal shots on target. Their defensive structure held firm, limiting Standard's ability to create clear-cut chances despite the home side's territorial dominance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players at half-time
halftimeSections.text: For KAA Gent, Wilfried Kanga stands out as the pivotal figure with two well-taken goals in the first half, showcasing his threat in front of goal. His impact has been decisive, supported by creative contributions from teammates like Abdelkahar Kadri and Atsuki Ito, who provided assists. On the Standard Liège side, players like Teddy Teuma have been instrumental in creating opportunities, while Daan Dierckx and Nayel Mehssatou showed promise in attack. However, the home team will need a clinical finisher to step up in the second half to overturn the deficit.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides, with Standard Liège often dictating the pace early on through sustained attacking spells. They frequently pushed forward, especially in the middle and latter stages of the half, showing determination to break through Gent's defense. However, KAA Gent had crucial moments of sharpness, particularly around the times of their goals, where they shifted momentum decisively in their favor. These brief but effective attacking bursts proved to be the difference, while Standard struggled to maintain consistent pressure during key phases.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical outlook for second half
halftimeSections.text: Standard Liège will likely come out with increased intensity in the second half, focusing on converting their territorial advantage into goals. They may push more players forward, potentially exploiting set-pieces or crosses given their aerial strength. KAA Gent, with a two-goal cushion, might adopt a more defensive approach, looking to absorb pressure and hit on the counter. Their strategy will hinge on maintaining composure and leveraging Kanga's finishing ability on any breakaway opportunities. Tactical adjustments from both managers will be critical in shaping the remainder of this match.
